name: Dumbbell Jefferson Curl
resistance_profile: Loads the erectors eccentrically through full segmental spinal flexion; tension is highest at the fully rounded, deeply stretched bottom position.
cues: Move slowly, one spinal segment at a time
Keep the weight light and dragging close to the legs
Restack the spine from the bottom up
execution_steps: Stand on a bench or box holding a light dumbbell in both hands, feet together and legs nearly straight.
Tuck the chin and slowly roll down one vertebra at a time, letting the spine flex segmentally.
Let the dumbbell travel along the front of the legs until you reach a full but comfortable stretch.
Reverse the motion, restacking the spine from the bottom up until you are standing tall.
good_when: You want to train the erectors through a full flexion range to build tissue tolerance
You have healthy spinal ROM and want a slow, controlled mobility-strength movement
bad_when: You have an acute disc injury or pain with loaded spinal flexion
You cannot control the load slowly — this is a light, deliberate lift, not a heavy one
common_errors: Using too much load and losing segmental control
Bouncing at the bottom instead of owning the stretch
Hinging only at the hips rather than flexing the spine
progressions: Increase range by standing on a taller box
regressions: Reduce range with a partial roll-down
